reviews.review_text:
Can't believe its almost 17 years since the release. Internet and the gaming landscape was so different back then, its almost bittersweet to remember those times. 

They don't make games like this anymore :/

[h1] One word describes this game: PEAK. [/h1]

Story: 8/10✅
    The narrative is gripping, but it leaves something to be desired. After defeating the final boss, the game cuts abruptly to the credits with only a brief audio clip from Batman. It lacks a proper epilogue or post-credits sequence, which feels a bit rushed—perhaps due to budget constraints at the time.

Gameplay: 10/10✅
    The game design is, without exaggeration, phenomenal. Every level is uniquely crafted; from the structural environment to enemy placement, everything is exactly where it needs to be to provide a perfect experience.

Sound & Music: 7.5/10✅
    The music is solid, though the sound design occasionally suffers from bugs when switching levels (which a quick reload usually fixes). However, the voice acting needs no introduction: with the legendary Kevin Conroy and Mark Hamill, every Batman and Joker fan is in for an absolute treat.

Overall:
Batman: Arkham Asylum remains one of the most atmospheric superhero games to this day—at times, it feels more like a survival horror game than a standard action title. It is truly a timeless masterpiece, and looking at the current state of Rocksteady, it’s honestly heartbreaking to see how far they’ve fallen from this level of quality.

The FreeFlow combat is incredibly satisfying and set the standard for action games. The dark, gothic atmosphere of the Asylum is brilliantly designed, the music and sound effects are great, and the legendary voice acting by Kevin Conroy and Mark Hamill is absolute perfection. It hasn’t aged a day. An absolute must-play A masterpiece

Batman Arkham Asylum still holds up incredibly well, especially for a game from 2009. The combat system is smooth, satisfying, and makes you feel like Batman without being overly complicated. I was also surprised by how good the graphics and overall presentation still look today.

What stood out to me most was the atmosphere of Arkham Asylum itself. The game really captures the dark Batman feeling, and exploring the asylum made the world feel memorable rather than just like a simple action game. I also enjoyed the Riddler challenges because they gave me a reason to explore more carefully and pay attention to the details.

Overall, I enjoyed the entire experience. It has great combat, strong atmosphere, fun side challenges, and it is easy to see why this game is still considered one of the best superhero games. I would definitely recommend it to Batman fans and action-adventure players.

Game Completion: ✅

The game is awesome, not sure why It took me so many restarts to finally give the game a proper playthrough but I've finally done it. I am starting to see why the arkham games are given such high praise as this was a very enjoyable experience. Now, I have fully completed the story; the final things that I am missing from my playthrough are achievements tied to collectables and challenge mode which I may attempt eventually, but for now I am wanting to try the next game in the series.

Very fun, I recommend

The absolute blueprint for modern combat and stealth.This is the gold standard. The FreeFlow melee is brutally satisfying, but the real highlight is the predator rooms. Stalking armed thugs from the gargoyles and dismantling them tactically one by one makes you feel like an absolute apex predator. It leans heavily into a dark, claustrophobic, almost survival-horror atmosphere—the Scarecrow sections alone are legendary.  If you are an achievement hunter, this is a phenomenal game to master. Tracking down all 240 Riddler trophies and grinding out the high scores in the combat challenges for the true 100% is incredibly rewarding. It doesn't waste your time with a bloated open world; it's just a tight, perfectly paced gothic masterpiece.Still plays flawlessly today. 10/10.

Batman: Arkham Asylum was at its peak, and to this day it’s still at its peak. I had so much fun playing this game, but the one major complaint I had with it was that it felt a little too simple at times. Of course, when making the first game in a series, some features are going to feel underdeveloped or not fully utilized, and that’s completely fine, especially for the first installment.
Even though Asylum is simple, that doesn’t make it bad. It just means the combat, detective work, bosses, and riddles had room to expand and improve in future games — and they definitely did in later game.
Overall, even with its simpler combat, bosses, riddles, and mechanics, Arkham Asylum is still an amazing superhero game, and I highly recommend it. I can’t wait to try City and Knight.

Thank you for reading and be safe.

As the first game in the series this is by far the simplest and smallest of the Arkham games. In my mind this makes it the perfect entry in the series to 100% as the amount of content is just big enough to justify going through the effort.

The combat and stealth are slightly jankier than the other entries, but the atmosphere it top notch with an excellent story this entry is a definite recommendation.

Great game! It really holds up well even after all these years. I love the voice actors and the story really makes you feel like your Batman. LOTS of times in vents, so much so that I feel Batman could've been a HVAC Tech in another life, but I'm not complaining lol 

If you're interested in taking a crack at the Batman series; don't skip out on this game! It's an all-time banger!

My first Batman game, and honestly, I loved it. The atmosphere is amazing, Joker is entertaining throughout, and the game constantly keeps you engaged. The combat was fun once I got used to it, and moments like Scarecrow's sequences, Bane, and Croc's lair were really memorable.

My only real issue was getting lost a few times because the map and objectives can be confusing. The final Joker boss fight was also a bit underwhelming compared to Poison Ivy.

Despite that, I couldn't stop playing. I spent 22 hours in Arkham and even watched the credits till the end.

Rating: 9/10. A memorable game that still holds up today. 🦇🔥

Really fun game to play as Batman and deploy his gadgets on the criminals of gotham. I can see why it got the GOTY award. 9/10. 1 point deducted for the game being short, plus the boss fights aren't impressive and also really less boss fights.
Very solid Batman game, story was enjoyable, combat was fun and made me feel unstopabble.
it was very fun to play, even to this day its a fun open world game and titan jokers design makes me speechless
Combat was extremely clunky but the game was overall fun.

I enjoyed Asylum, the story is pretty basic and the game world small, but the combat and boss fights are fun and occasionally challenging. Some stealth parts were not very intuitive to understand, but I enjoyed most of them anyway!
This game was the reason I bought an Xbox360 back in 2009. It is incredible, I love it and yes, it does in fact make you feel like you are Batman. What else could you want?

If your a player who listens to the game music or not, I would always recommend playing this game with all its audio and visual effects so really experience the vibe of arkham asylum. This game is a masterpiece for its time in 2009.  If you are planning on playing any of the arkham games, the best game to start with is arkham asylum as it shows you how this was the baseline for arkham city and arkham knight. Yes I do agree that compared to the other three arkham games this is not that great, but as a first timer for the Arkham series this is totally the vibe. I want to explain more but I don't want to give away any exciting spoilers for the people who wish to play this game. Just give it a shot and complete the main storyline in normal difficulty. If you enjoy it then complete it again on hard difficulty and try to 100% the game, which would only take about 40 hours. If you even do half of the challenge mode I'm telling you man your combat and predator skills will improve to a whole new level, and if you want to go the extremes and master predator challenges with 3 batarangs in the quickest way possible, I would recommend using the help of youtube tutorials which shows you how creative you can be in both stealth and combat. Doing all of this will grant you an amazing  introduction to the arkham series and go hit Arkham City like it was your workout after your warm up.

It's aged well overall. Took about 15 hours to complete the campaign on normal difficulty and collect all Riddler trophies, though you may eke out some extra play time by playing on hard or completing the extra challenge missions.

Part beat 'em up, part metroid lite, part collectathon. Plenty of upgrades and some nice batman lore to find. Story is fine, but not particularly interesting. The score is nice. The gameplay laid a solid foundation for the series, with games still copying the combat system to this day. The attention to detail is something you still don't see in games today, with Batman growing five o'clock shadow and his suit getting more beat up as the game progresses.

The game momentarily stutters on occasion. Perhaps a dozen times over the course of the campaign.

It's a solid game that's well worth your time, whether you're a Batman fan or not.

Haven’t played this since around 2010, but coming back to it brought back a lot of fun memories. The experience still holds up really well overall, though my opinion of the final boss hasn’t changed. it’s pretty underwhelming.
Great game. the freeflow combat is awesome, the stealth is awesome. the story while a bit out there, its still awesome. 10/10

Not gonna lie, before playing this game I thought that, since it was released in 2009, the graphics would feel outdated and the gameplay would be nothing special. I was completely wrong.

The graphics are way ahead of their time. I genuinely can't imagine a game looking this good back in 2009. Even today, some aspects of the visuals and atmosphere still hold up surprisingly well.

The story is fantastic, the Riddler challenges are fun, and the light parkour/traversal elements make exploring Arkham Island enjoyable. The game design is, without exaggeration, phenomenal. Every area feels carefully crafted; from the environment design to enemy placement, everything is exactly where it needs to be to create a great experience.

One thing that really impressed me was the physics. The papers scattered around the ground react to your movement and the air you create while running past them. It sounds like a small detail, but it's incredibly immersive. Honestly, some modern AAA games still don't pay this much attention to environmental interaction.

The Scarecrow sections completely blew my mind. I genuinely cannot imagine how the developers came up with those psychological nightmare sequences back in 2009. The environment constantly changes around you, reality starts breaking apart, and the game makes you question what's actually happening. It felt unique, creative, and unlike anything I had experienced before. The boss fights themselves are not just about combat; they're about atmosphere, tension, and messing with the player's mind.

The death cutscenes are also a brilliant touch. Instead of being frustrating, they are entertaining and add personality to the villains.

Amazing story, great gameplay, excellent atmosphere, incredible attention to detail, and some of the most creative level design I've seen in a game.

10/10. A timeless classic.

This game crawled so the others in the series, Arkham City specifically, can run.

For the first in the series of the Arkham games it is really good despite not having too much content. I completed this game in about 32 hours worth of gameplay and was fun the whole step of the way. 

The way the game registers counter attacks is very clunky and thank goddess they fixed it in the later games.

4.5/5
Its truly valuable and nice but I can say it has its own problems such as slow navigation and narrition in some points and also confusing minor bugs and stuff.
wow, a mystery thriller/horror with one of the most satisfying combat system. perfect for a batman game.
great game but use legacy physx driver to fix rendering thread exception error
this game so goooooooooood it gets everything right i love the story i love the combat the stealth missions are amazing everything is amaaaaazing

Did anyone catch the game last night?

For a game that came out in 2009, this is really enjoyable. I never 100% games but because I love batman since like 2010 I couldn't resist this game. Listening to the batman 1989 iconic theme while beating up some arkham inmates was very fun. Took me ages to get 100% on all combat challenges but it was so satisfying when I completed them. Can't wait to play Arkham City!!!!! :)

Fun Batman game overall. It really has it all, scary stuff, nice combat system, and interesting humor. Achievement grinding wasn't too bad unless you kinda suck at combat, but all you need to do is lock in and hope you don't get slapped by anybody 🙏
At First i didn't like this game because of how old compared to it's successors, but I came to apreciate how simple yet enjoyable it is.
Without a doubt the best Batman game. Some of the most fluid freeform combat I've ever experienced in a game. Plus, Kevin Conroy's Batman and Mark Hamill's Joker are always a treat.

un juegazo con todas las letras.
los gráficos, integración con la historia, cinemáticas, todo. increíble.

In consideration of how old the game is, it was actually really fun. What makes it fun is the voice acting and the general care that is put into these games. Story was amazing as well. With that said, i think this was the worst in the series. Which makes sense since it was the first. Still holds up fine grafically, it could use a remaster or a remake to make it up to modern standards though.

This game is truly amazing. Just unlocked all achievements. Feels great to finish it. Yeah. Hope everyone reading this post has enjoyed it as well. (Except for obtaining the medals in predator encounters with the booby trapped vantage points!!!)
The hardest one in the trilogy (on hard mode). No counter indicators, punches hurt even with max armor, and not a lot combat and predator options that the next 2 games gave (origins is not a game)
Good pacing. Nice balance of detective/problem solving and stealth and frontal combat.

First released in 2009, I played it at launch on the Xbox 360; 17 years later, I decided to replay it on PC, and it still holds up in every way. Arkham Asylum is easily one of the best games ever made and is still worth full price, period.

SCORE: 10/10.

Beat this game a long time ago on Xbox 360 and tend to go back to it every now and then. From the Combat to the story, it set a precedent on how super hero games should be. Still one of the best super hero games out there and i highly recommend it.

It's crazy to think that this was a game released for the PS3 and the Xbox 360 back in 2009. Unreal Engine used to be something else back then. It's crazy how good this game looks to this day. I bought this game along with Arkham City and Arkham Knight for cheap. I'll highly recommend this game

Very successful for its time, and the fact that it revolutionized the combat system is a whole other matter. It is one of the games that shaped the gaming world. Its atmosphere and combat system are beautiful, and its story is decent.
good game, one of the best superhero video games of all time, arguably better that the Spiderman games.

Solid start to the franchise. The bosses are very repetitive and uninteresting and I don't like how there's no i-frames for the long takedown animations. Other than that, gameplay outside of those two issues was engaging enough that I was never bored while playing through it
this is a perfect i mean perfect batman game everything is perfect other than the joker fight, i loved the scarecrow fight it was so creative and so good.
One of the most well paced video games ever made. Feel like you are not just the batman but in a Batman comic book. 10/10 game, hell the whole franchise!

Batman Arkham Asylum is a near perfect game. Asylum has some of the best atmosphere, combat, and story you'd find in a game. As for some of the criticism, boss fights can be very underwhelming and easy, however, the bosses' personalities makes up for the lack of difficulty--each boss has their own gimmick. As for the collectables, contrary to popular belief, I found them very enjoyable. Getting all achievements was a nice challenge that didn't feel grindy.

This game turned me into a completionist. So refreshing for a game to have side content that doesn't feel like a chore to pursue.

And dat combat system! Timeless!!!

If mixed was possible I would give it mixed as I don't think it is bad enough to warrant a dislike nor good enough to warrant a like.

This game is a story based, action, soft-puzzle game. The story is fun to follow and quite frequent, which is the saving grace of this game. Because the rest is mostly negatives

- You spend 95% of the fights fighting henchmen, yes, hundreds of them. I played the game on hard mode due to the difficulty based achievement so maybe it's different on easier difficulties but to be swarmed by dozen henchmen that all take 3-5 hits to go down over and over again is not fun
- You have three attacks. The regular attacks, counter (and counter attack), batarang. Your regular attacks deal little damage but it's decently fast, your counter attack would work if it had some kind of iframe but it doesn't so most of the time you will have to counter another attack instead of chaining into a counter attack as you are getting swarmed by multiple enemies and they don't wait their turn
- There are 5 proper boss fights in this game. They are all gimmicky, but 2 of them are 100% gimmick. One of them is just a platformer with forced camera angle where you play hide and seek and reach the end, one is a platformer where you are forced to silently walk else you die except when you get caught anyway so you have to run sometimes. One is dodge, throw batarang, dodge throw batarang where you cant interact with the boss in any other way. One is the same except you can interact with the boss after you throw the batarang. The latter 2 also summon tons of henchman at you. What does it mean? Now you spend most of your turn fighting meaningless mobs instead of the actual boss because they stagger you on hit and you have to defend against their attacks too. No i didn't miss the fifth, because the fifth is just a henchman battle. He summons mobs, you fight them, every now and then you can interact with the boss, he loses 1/3 health, you repeat it three times.
In short: The fighting in this game is alright, but the actual fighting sequence is consistently horrible. If you like fighting AS batman (with his extremely basic skillset) then you will get a lot of it, if you like fighting AS batman AGAINST other DC heroes you won't find it here.
- At the end of each battle (including mob fights that are everywhere) there is forced kill cam where you just punch or kick as it that's cool to look at and not just a waste of time
- There are hundreds of collectibles in this game, they give you exp so if you want levels to unlock abilities you need to gather them, they aren't as mandatory on easier difficulties but on hard mode I really made use of especially defense ones. I collected about 75% of the collectibles and my achievement progress is 32/47, why? Because save for 1 or 2 they are collectible or "challenge" based achivements. If you want to 100% this game you have to grind it, you can't just play the game itself well.
- As you go through the game you unlock more and more tools, even at the last 10% of the game you unlock another one. And guess what? You have to return to places you have already been to and use these newfound tools to reach new areas and collect the rest of the collectibles. It doesn't contain a fast travel system either, you are forced to backtrack

I wanted to play a story based game, and a DC game cuz I am a stranger to that universe. The game provides you that well. I don't regret playing this game and you probably won't either but know that it's not an overwhelmingly positive 96% game. I will vote this down to skew the results towards realism
Fun game , short play through relative to the amount of time you have quite enjoyable
Classic Batman game many of the 360 era grew up with. Still holds up, but is starting to feel dated.

This isn't just a generic superhero action game; it is a top-tier psychological thriller. The atmosphere inside the Asylum is incredibly dark, creepy, and claustrophobic. The way the game uses its adaptive audio design and the constant eerie intercom announcements by the Joker creates an intense layer of anxiety and immersion . one of the best game of superhero
Can't believe that this game is still relevant after 17 YEARS. #GOATED STORYLINE#. It is like an old wine which gets better and better..
"This game is freaking awesome. Probably my favorite game in the Arkham series, even though some of the challenges made me want to pull my hair out.
Amazing game would recommend, so good i even got all those damn Riddler trophies, though it was fun, had a good time, while the other games are better
The game that started it all. Despite not having all the innovations in the later games, it is still so much fun to play, and it's atmosphere and pacing is the best out of all of them.

Well....... I haven't this game for long but this game is definitely reaaly really good and ummmm I don't know why but it crashes many times before starting there's the main issue there but like apart from that the game is really top-notch and i particularly like the combat in the game
best game from xbox 360 era and classic gameplay and the map can be overwhelming sometimes but a  10/10  experience but batmobile is icionic
This is a great game that set a standard for superhero and OpenWorld games alike.
best fight sequences and bosses after city

man this game is so peak. i love its linear gameplay and the atmosphere is just so eerie. it sometimes starts to feel like no less than a horror game. straight 10/10 (if we ignore the final boss fight)

damn i really didnt expect this game to be this good. even tho the bost fight sometimes can be repetitive as in the way to defeat them over all this game is amazing especially for the time it was release. i greatly recommended it! 
8,5/10

The BEST game series ever. I LOVE this game. I CANT STOP playing this GAME. If you have not PLAYED this GAME you SHOULD play it. I WISH there were MORE games of this SERIES. WHY did they STOP MAKING these GAMES?

Batman: Arkham Asylum is a masterpiece; it created the free-flow combat in 2009 and defined what superhero games should be, and later refined the combat throughout the games. It balances combat and predator perfectly, with the different moves that Batman can use to get through these sections of the game.  Batman’s and The Joker’s dynamic is so good as Joker takes control of the Asylum and talks to Batman through the PA, keeping the Gothic tone with inspirations from A Serious House on A Serious Earth.
 
Where the setting is set out perfectly for what Rocksteady created the game to be more of a Metroidvania, by going back to previous locations of the Asylum for story and collectables to take down The Riddler. The Asylum feels alive as it makes you feel unnerved with some of the locations like The Medical Facility, The Sewers and The Botanical Gardens that set the atmosphere that makes it feel darker than it is from a Pegi 16, expanding the lore of the game with different character bios and stories that make the game feel alive, which the bios get retconned.
  
The game makes you feel like prey and also a predator as you're trapped with them, but they are trapped with you as well.  His villains challenge him mentally and physically, with The Scarecrow segments being last-minute additions, exploring his grief over his parents and what it means to be Batman and the temptation of Poison Ivy, leading him to finally face Joker, leading him to break Batman, but he has the mental fortitude to beat him as they both need each other to co-exist.  Creating a Big Bang.

“Welcome to the Madhouse, Batman!”
Very good , Enjoyed the mechanics but would have been good if the tools animation didn't take 50 ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ years. Enjoyed it

This doesn't look and play like a 2009 game. Rocksteady created a banger, and Joker is HILARIOUS! I enjoyed listening to Villains interview tapes. I have come to conclusion that the real bad guys in Gotham are its corrupt establishment whose actions created these villains.
Peak Batman game. Combat 10/10, Music 8/10, Story 9/10, Boss-fights 6/10 (somewhat boring once you get used to combat moves)
Such an amazing game. The first Arkham game in the series and its so amazing. I love Batman.

If anyone is interested, the latest Nvidia Gameready drivers for 50 series Blackwell cards now support the 32 bit Physx driver implementation that Nvidia announced last year and it seems to be working properly in Arkham Asylum, I encountered no slowdowns whatsoever beyond entering new areas which seems to be more an Unreal Engine 3 issue. 

Still a classic game from the PS3 era, and worth a replay for sure.
amazing game used to crash but after repair ran great and was incredibly fun
pretty good game with really good atmosphere and they did made a pretty amazing joker i really liked the game
lots of years later still have a very good gameplay and story.

The game that started it all — the first entry in the series that would later become known as the “Arkham Trilogy.” I have fond memories of playing this game back in early 2020, before the pandemic. At the time, I only focused on the linear story narrative and never attempted a 100% completion run.

Coming back to it in 2026, I started a fresh save file and fully committed to completing the game and earning all the achievements. The hardest part of the achievement hunt was definitely the challenge maps — especially the Shock & Awe (Extreme) challenge.

Overall, this game is a reminder of what truly makes a game amazing. All in all, I’d highly recommend it. It also runs really well on the Steam Deck.

Legacy content game today but goty for a good reason. Game has perfect balance between solving riddles and running around the Arkam asylum and combat. It has complexitiy in charachters and in story telling and each charachter is unique in it own way. In 4k game looks like its not that old with good visuals and eaven better athosphere. Cutscnes are good and reward when u complete a chapter and progress and they add special momentum to gamplay. Any gpu today can run it in 4k all max.

There’s something special about coming back to Batman: Arkham Asylum after all these years. What once felt groundbreaking somehow still holds up incredibly well today.

The atmosphere is unmatched. From the moment Joker arrives at Arkham, the game pulls you into its dark, tense world and never really lets go. The asylum itself almost feels like a character, packed with detail, creepy environments, and constant tension around every corner.

Combat still feels smooth and satisfying, especially considering how many modern games were inspired by it. Predator sections remain some of the best stealth gameplay in any superhero title, and the voice acting from Kevin Conroy and Mark Hamill is legendary.

What really hits the hardest replaying it now is the nostalgia. This was the game that proved superhero games could actually be amazing. It set the standard for an entire generation.

Even in 2026, Batman: Arkham Asylum is still an unforgettable experience and absolutely worth revisiting.

The atmosphere is perfect — gothic, dark, pure Batman.
The Arkham games were clearly made with love, and you can feel it even today.
So many modern games lack the passion and attention to detail the Arkham trilogy has.
As a marvel fan this is my first time playing a DC character inspired game and I love the game so far,  Truly makes me feel like Batman.
great game ngl i completed all riddle and it was worth it. this game vibe is so good ngl

Batman: Arkham Asylum = fantastic game 
Due to my low end laptop specs its not been luxury for me to play good games, but games like this are literally make you feel old=gold, nice story,  nice character great experience

The game is pretty old, graphics and gameplay mechanics aren't the best. But its worth every mini-second!! the storyline, battles, boss fights, especially how intimidating it will start to feel as you get deeper in the story... cuz of the insanity!
Its a bit buggy but i cant really say anything just because its old the combos is very interesting but citys better but yeah overall a great game.

This game aged like the finest wine. The visuals, level design, combat system, story, voice acting, art style - everything is amazing. The gameplay is less diverse than in later titles, but still extremely enjoyable. Being the only Arkham title with Metroidvania level design, it provides a unique experience. Clearing secondary content (riddles and challenges) is also fun and doesn't feel like generic slop of the modern games. I had a blast with it on the Steam Deck. It feels like it was so much ahead of its time in 2009. A masterpiece!

Arkham Asylum remains a tightly paced masterpiece that traps you in a terrifying Gothic madhouse. The game revolutionized rhythmic FreeFlow combat and stealthy Predator encounters, making you truly feel like the Dark Knight as you dismantle the Joker's thugs. Navigating the intricately connected map of Arkham Island reveals brilliantly designed locations, like the Intensive Treatment center and Botanical Gardens, filled with hidden Riddler quests and lore. As you progress, you continuously upgrade your Batsuit armor and unlock iconic weapons and gadgets, such as the explosive gel and Batclaw, to access new areas and conquer escalating threats. Anchored by legendary voice performances from Kevin Conroy and Mark Hamill, it stands as an undeniable triumph of gaming history.
i played this game for way to long and loves it but hated it at the same time cuz poison ivy is way to hard..............

A fantastic introduction to the series, it's surprising how well this game holds up, yeah the combat con be repetitive but it's crunchy and satisfaying, and the main reason to play the game is for it's world building, many characters and cameos and how right they got Batman here.

It really makes you feel like Batman

 8/10

First series of batman you must start the game triology from this.

Storyline : 9/10
Gameplay :10/10 this game was awesome mechanic gameplay
Achievement 100% : Super Fun eventhough its hard
Price :10/10 Cheap for long game like this

Overall i rate this game 9/10, its really awesome game

I got this game a while ago on sale along with the Arkham City and Arkham Knight. I hope the story on those other games are as engaging as this game....I really didn't want it to end and I wish it was a little longer but I finished it already and have found over 70% of the hidden Riddler items etc.  I know this game has been out for some time but If you love Batman and all the villains associated with him then getting this game is a no brainer!!

The game just refuses to launch for me, i purchased this months ago and still have yet to see the main menu. I did not refund it since i got it on sale i thought i could fix it and because i didnt know whether i would get such a deal again, but man, i have been troubleshooting for ages and still stuck. Download DirectX9, NET framework, and everything, but it just refuses to launch. This isnt just me too, ive seen several people complaining about the same issue. 

Ik i can attach the specs but im noting them down to add more -
Ryzen 7 5700X
RX 9060 XT 16gb VRAM
2x16gb DDr4 3200Mhz CL16
Windows 11 
Fully Updated drivers, directX, visual c++, .NET framework, etc.
Not to mention my pc is relatively new (about half a year old)
Story rich and fluid gameplay

Does not feel like that old of a game. The combat system is smooth, the graphics are decent, the story is a bit cliche, but oh well. The immersion is top-notch; my favorite part was detective mode and the stealth challenges, the AI is pretty good in those. The only slight downside imo would be that the boss fights are a bit too gimmicky. 

Oh also, pro: Poison Ivy exists.
Great game, great story, great art
The music, the characters, the ambiance. This game just oozes with aura.
Decent combat for an old game. Pretty fun game to 100% but the last boss is pretty underwhelming and the challenges can get repetitive.
The OG and the best batman Arkham series game ever. The story and the jokar . I loved it

16 Years on and this game is still great and looks great.
PhysX is a pain to get working these days but it's pretty nice.
The way I got it to work:
Install the Redist PhysX from the game files
Then Install Legacy PhysX from the Nvidia website
IN THAT ORDER
I used the advanced launcher mod from Nexus and only ever booted the game through Nexus.
Booting the game from Steam messed up the PhysX and wouldn't boot without reinstalling them both again.

After playing Batman: Arkham Asylum, I can say it still feels like one of the best superhero games ever made. The dark atmosphere of Arkham Island is amazing and keeps the game intense from start to finish. Combat feels smooth and satisfying, especially when chaining attacks and countering enemies like Batman in the movies. The stealth sections are also very fun and make you feel powerful and smart at the same time. The story is engaging, with Joker being one of the best parts of the game, and the voice acting makes every scene more enjoyable. Even though the game is older, the gameplay and atmosphere still hold up really well today, making it a must-play for Batman fans and newcomers alike.
I come back to replay this & all the Arkham games & it's a 10/10 This one is the most camp out of the 4 games but the atmosphere & ambiance is probably the best!

I am and sick and tired of narrative driven boss battles.. Why, you can kick the ass of any boss,,   Can we please have a developer that makes a battle driven by the skill of the player and the skills they provide instead of this tiresome, boring, do this, now do this..  I dont care.. Give me control..  Youve made a game great, a game not the movie you wanted to make,, Give me that game and control not your wanton movie.

The comat in this game is excellent , the riddles are creative , i love how the story goes , batman's ass has a lot of details , i got this game with a bundle for arkham collection for 9$ and i do not regret any action this was worth every tf2 key i sold to get this game , one of the best games especially with optimizations , i did not expect a game like this with high graphics to run on  bad pcs.
The perfect "closed-map" superhero game. Incredible atmosphere.

For a 2000s game, it holds up very well. It's a classic, super enjoyable, and very puzzling that will have you caught on most of the time. It's definetley one of the best super hero games of all time. 8/10
The game was grate, played it after several years and still an play without getting bored

An absolute classic, a staple of superhero video games, and a must play for every superhero, batman fan, and gamers in general. I loved it years ago, i love it now, i loved it on my xbox and i love it on my pc.
While in some ways its not aged the best, the game is still a work of art everyone should at least try.
